description: sovereign state in West Africa
description_wiki: Nigeria, officially the Federal Republic of Nigeria, is a country in West Africa between the Sahel to the north and the Gulf of Guinea in the Atlantic Ocean to the south.

description: American daily newspaper (founded 1851)
description_wiki: The New York Times (NYT) is a newspaper based in Manhattan, New York City.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: liberal arts work college in Kentucky, USA
description_wiki: Berea College is a private liberal arts work college in Berea, Kentucky, United States.

description: 1861–1865 civil war in the United States between the North and the South over the enslavement of African-Americans
description_wiki: The American Civil War (April 12, 1861 – May 26, 1865; also known by other names) was a civil war in the United States between the Union ("the North") and the Confederacy ("the South"), which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States, which they saw as threatened because of the election of Abraham Lincoln and the growing abolitionist movement in the North.

description: United States civil rights organization
description_wiki: The Congress of Racial Equality (CORE) is an African-American civil rights organization in the United States that played a pivotal role for African Americans in the civil rights movement.

description: Confederate States general (1807–1870)
description_wiki: Robert Edward Lee (January 19, 1807 – October 12, 1870) was a Confederate general whose early actions in the American Civil War led to his appointment as the overall commander of the Confederate States Army near the end of the war.

description: capital city of Virginia, United States of America
description_wiki: Richmond (, RITCH-mənd) is the capital city of the U.S. state of Virginia.

description: president of the United States from 1869 to 1877
description_wiki: Ulysses S. Grant (born Hiram Ulysses Grant; April 27, 1822 – July 23, 1885) was the 18th president of the United States, serving from 1869 to 1877.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Texas (  TEK-səss) is the most populous state in the Southern United States.

description: holiday commemorating the emancipation of enslaved African Americans in Texas on June 19, 1865
description_wiki: Juneteenth, officially Juneteenth National Independence Day, is a federal holiday in the United States.

description: city in and county seat of Galveston County, Texas, United States; port and coastal city
description_wiki: Galveston ( GAL-vis-tən) is a resort city and port on the Gulf Coast of the U.S. state of Texas.

description: Union Army General (1821–1876)
description_wiki: Gordon Granger (November 6, 1821 – January 10, 1876) was a career U.S. Army officer, and a Union general during the American Civil War, where he distinguished himself at the Battle of Chickamauga.

description: private research university in Stanford, California, USA
description_wiki: Leland Stanford Junior University, commonly referred to as Stanford University, is a private research university in Stanford, California, United States.

surface_forms: the foothills of the Appalachian Mountains
description: mountain range in the eastern United States and Canada
description_wiki: The Appalachian Mountains, often called the Appalachians, are a mountain range in eastern to northeastern North America.

description: river in Kentucky, United States
description_wiki: The Kentucky River is a tributary of the Ohio River in Kentucky, United States.

description: public university in the United States
description_wiki: The University of Kentucky (UK, UKY, or U of K) is a public land-grant research university in Lexington, Kentucky, United States.
